This PR was generated by the branch-cut automation workflow.

Master-side partner PR for the freshly-cut `<REL_BRANCH>` branch (target version `<VERSION>`). Paired with a rel-side `branch-cut/<REL_BRANCH>` PR that prepares the new rel branch itself. See [`docs/release-mechanism-gaps.md` (G4 + G5)][gaps] and [`docs/release-mechanism-migration-from-devops.md` (workstream 2 detail)][plan] for the design.

[gaps]: ../../docs/release-mechanism-gaps.md
[plan]: ../../docs/release-mechanism-migration-from-devops.md

> **Do not push commits directly to this PR's branch (`branch-cut/<REL_BRANCH>-master`).**
> The workflow may re-run after fixes; manual commits could be overwritten.
> If a mechanical change here is wrong, fix it at the source (mutator code on master),
> merge that fix, then re-run the branch-cut workflow via `workflow_dispatch`.

## What this PR covers (master-side, next-dev advance)

- **Docker upgrade scaffolding** — same docker-version bump + new `fsupgrade-N.sh` stub + Dockerfile manifest update as the rel-side PR (cross-branch sync requirement per the `docker upgrade actions mandatory per release` rule).
- **SQL upgrade skeleton** — creates `sql/<from>-to-<to>_upgrade.sql` stub (header only; per-release work fills the body).
- **`version.php`** — bumps from `8.M.0-dev` to `8.(M+1).0-dev` so master keeps marking development builds for the next minor line.
- **`src/RestControllers/OpenApi/OpenApiDefinitions.php`** — bumps `OA\Info(version: ...)` to the new master version.
- **`swagger/openemr-api.yaml`** — regenerated from `OpenApiDefinitions.php` via `openemr:create-api-documentation`.
- **`.github/release-targets.yml`** — inserts the new rel row (`docker_tags: <VERSION>,next`), bumps the master row's docker_tags (minor++, drops `next`, keeps `dev`), and removes any rows previously flagged `unreleased: true` (covers the skip-line-cut scenario uniformly).

## Lifecycle

1. Workflow fires on `create` event for the new `rel-NNN0` branch (or via `workflow_dispatch` for recovery).
2. This master-side PR opens as a draft alongside the rel-side PR.
3. Maintainer reviews both, marks Ready, and merges in order: rel-side first, then master-side.

## Release manager checklist

- [ ] Confirm `version.php` advanced to the expected next-dev version.
- [ ] Confirm the new SQL upgrade skeleton filename matches the version pair (e.g., `8_2_0-to-8_3_0_upgrade.sql`).
- [ ] Verify the `release-targets.yml` diff: new rel row present with `<VERSION>,next`, master row bumped to next minor with `dev` only.
- [ ] Confirm the paired `branch-cut/<REL_BRANCH>` (rel-side) PR opened and merged first.
- [ ] Merge this PR (master-side).
